subject: Business License Renewal — Sobczak Alterations, 3408 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing to confirm that my business license renewal for Sobczak Alterations was received by the Clerk's office last Thursday. I dropped it off with payment in person and was told processing takes up to three weeks. My current license expires February 28th and I'm worried about a lapse. I've been at 3408 Washington Blvd for eleven years without any compliance issues. Is there any way to prioritize my application given the tight window?

subject: Business License Renewal — Sobczak Alterations, 3408 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Ms. Sobczak, thank you for reaching out and for your eleven years of business in Bellwood. I confirmed with the Clerk's office that your renewal is in the queue. Given your expiration date, I've flagged it for priority review — you should receive the renewed license by February 25th at the latest. If you haven't heard back by the 24th, please call us directly at 708-555-0141 and ask for me. We appreciate your patience.

subject: Opening New BBQ Restaurant on St. Charles — Questions About Licensing
body_clean: Mayor, I'm planning to open Webb's Smokehouse at 528 St. Charles Rd in the old dry cleaner space. Super excited — been dreaming about this for fifteen years. Before I sign the lease I want to understand what licenses and permits I'm looking at. I know there's a business license, but what about food service, live music some weekends, and maybe outdoor seating in warmer months? Would love to schedule a pre-application meeting with whoever handles new businesses.

subject: Opening New BBQ Restaurant on St. Charles — Questions About Licensing
body_clean: Marcus, welcome — this is exactly the kind of investment we want on St. Charles. You're right that there are several layers: business license, Cook County health department food service permit, and depending on amplification, a live entertainment conditional-use approval through the Village Board. Outdoor seating triggers a sidewalk-cafe permit and an encroachment agreement with Public Works. I'm copying Tina Alvarez who can set up a pre-application meeting with Community Development. Don't sign anything yet — let's talk first.

subject: Opening New BBQ Restaurant on St. Charles — Questions About Licensing
body_clean: Mayor, I really appreciate that response — much more thorough than I expected. I'll hold off on signing and reach out to Ms. Alvarez to get on the calendar. Happy to work through all the proper channels. This community deserves something great and I intend to deliver it.

subject: Sign Permit Status — Nair Yoga & Wellness, 4215 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Hello, I submitted sign permit application #SP-2024-0318 on October 3rd for a new exterior sign at my yoga studio on Bellwood Ave. The permit office told me four to six weeks, but I'm now at five weeks with no update. My sign company has materials ready and is booked for installation the first week of November — if the permit isn't approved by then I lose my installation slot until January. Could someone please check on the status?

subject: Sign Permit Status — Nair Yoga & Wellness, 4215 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Ms. Nair, I looked into SP-2024-0318 this morning. The application is in final review and the permit is expected to be issued by October 31st, ahead of your installation window. There was a brief hold while staff confirmed the sign dimensions against the corridor sign standards in the municipal code — that's been cleared. You'll receive an email notification from the permit system when it's ready for pickup. Thank you for your patience and congratulations on the studio.

subject: Outdoor Seating Permit Renewal — El Faro Cantina
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I want to start the process for renewing our outdoor seating permit for this spring and summer. Last year we had twelve tables on the St. Charles sidewalk under permit #OSP-2024-07 — no complaints and we stayed within the approved footprint the entire season. I'd like to add two tables this year if the clearance allows. Do I need a new encroachment agreement since I'm expanding the footprint, and what's the timeline for renewal?

subject: Outdoor Seating Permit Renewal — El Faro Cantina
body_clean: Ms. Vega, renewal is straightforward since you're a returning permit holder with a clean record. Adding two tables does require an amended encroachment agreement — we need to re-measure and document the sidewalk clear-path against the five-foot minimum. I can get a crew out to measure early next week. Once that's confirmed, Community Development turns the paperwork in about ten business days. File by April 1 and you'll be legal before your May 1 opening.

subject: Facade Grant Program — Is It Still Active?
body_clean: Mayor, I own a small barbershop in the storefront at 302 25th Ave. Another business owner told me Bellwood has a facade improvement grant but the Village website page looks outdated and I can't find current details. Is the program still running? If so, what are the eligibility limits, and can I apply for exterior painting and a new awning in the same application? I've been at this location six years and the storefront could really use some help.

subject: Vacant Storefront at 718 Mannheim Rd
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the storefront at 718 Mannheim Rd has been empty going on two years. Broken blinds in the window, a fading handwritten For Lease sign, and last month someone spray-painted the side door. I know Mannheim is a state route and maybe the Village's hands are tied on some things, but is there anything that can be done to attract a tenant or at least compel the owner to clean it up? It's dragging down that whole stretch.

subject: Annual License Renewal Submitted — Esposito's Auto Detailing
body_clean: Hi, just a quick note to confirm I submitted my annual business license renewal for Esposito's Auto Detailing at 3901 Bohland Ave online this past Friday. The portal gave me confirmation number BL-2025-7742, but I never received an email confirmation. The October 1 renewal deadline has me a little nervous. Did it go through okay? Thanks.

subject: New Flower Shop — Sidewalk Display Question
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, I'm opening Petal & Stem Floral next month at the corner of 50th Ave and Washington Blvd. My business license application is already in. What I haven't figured out is sidewalk displays — I'd like to put buckets of flowers and a small A-frame chalkboard sign out front during business hours, same as many flower shops do. Is that covered under the business license or do I need a separate permit? I want to start clean from day one.

subject: Business License Name Change — Osei Insurance Group, 2214 Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I've run an insurance agency at 2214 Marshall Ave since 2019. I recently rebranded from Osei Financial Services to Osei & Partners Insurance Group — same ownership, same address, same EIN, just a new DBA registered with the state. Do I need a brand-new business license in Bellwood or can my existing license be amended to show the new name? I'd like to avoid paying a full new-license fee if a simple name update is possible.

subject: Facade Grant Application FIG-2025-14 — December Agenda?
body_clean: Good afternoon. I submitted Facade Improvement Grant application FIG-2025-14 in November for my clothing boutique at 3317 St. Charles Rd. I'm hoping to replace the dated brown awning with a new black-and-gold canopy and repaint the storefront trim. I understand the review board meets monthly — will my application be on the December agenda or pushed to January? I want to time my contractor deposit accordingly.

subject: Late License Renewal Penalty — Szymanski Hardware, 4102 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I received a notice that my business license for Szymanski Hardware at 4102 Hirsch Ave is overdue and a $150 late penalty has been assessed. I want to be upfront: I missed the July 1 renewal date because I was dealing with a serious family medical situation. I've now submitted the renewal with full payment. I'm not disputing the renewal fee — only asking whether there's a process to appeal the penalty given the circumstances. I have documentation if needed.

subject: Sign Permit for New Nail Salon — 3620 Washington Blvd
body_clean: Hi Mayor's office, I'm opening a nail salon at 3620 Washington Blvd and need a sign permit for an illuminated channel-letter sign above the entrance — 32 inches tall, 12 feet wide. I want to confirm the size is within village limits before I order fabrication. Also, does an illuminated sign require a separate electrical inspection or is that part of the sign permit process? First time dealing with this and I don't want any surprises.

subject: License Address Change — Bellwood Print & Copy Moving to Eastern Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've been running Bellwood Print & Copy at 4418 Harvard Ave since 2021 and I'm moving to a bigger space at 3109 Eastern Ave in early December. Same business name, same ownership, same EIN — just a different address. Do I need to apply for a brand-new business license at the new address or can my existing license be updated? I want to be legal from the first day I open at the new location.

subject: Sidewalk Cafe Permit — Bellwood Breakfast Co., 3814 St. Charles Rd
body_clean: Good morning, this is our first summer in the new space on St. Charles and I'd like to put four small two-top tables outside on the sidewalk from 7 a.m. to 2 p.m., Tuesday through Sunday. I understand I need a sidewalk cafe permit. What do I need to submit and how long does approval take? I'm hoping to have tables out by the July 4th weekend if at all possible.

subject: Thank You — Facade Grant Approved for Monroe Tax Services
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I just received word that my Facade Improvement Grant for Monroe Tax Services at 4005 Washington Blvd was approved. I'm genuinely thrilled. The new green awning and fresh storefront paint are going to make such a difference — I've already lined up the contractor for November. I wanted to personally thank you and your team for making this program available. Small business owners depend on initiatives like this, and Bellwood is lucky to have leadership that invests in its commercial corridors.

subject: Home-Based Business License — Candles and Soaps, 1902 Granville Ave
body_clean: Hello, I recently started making and selling handmade candles and soaps from my home at 1902 Granville Ave — mostly online but sometimes at local pop-up markets. A friend said I might need a Village business license even though I'm not seeing customers at the house. I have no employees, no signage, and all the work is done quietly in my basement. Is a home occupation license required for something this small? I want to comply if so.

subject: Menu Board Sign — Do I Need a Permit?
body_clean: Hi Mayor's office. I own Taqueria Castillo at 2819 St. Paul Ave and I want to mount a large printed-vinyl menu board on the exterior wall next to my entrance — about 4 feet by 6 feet, not illuminated. My neighbor says anything on the outside of the building needs a sign permit. Is that really true for a menu board, or are permits only for lit signs? I don't want to spend money on printing until I know it'll pass.
